explicit_text: I currently live in Lexington, Kentucky, mainly because it's one of the most affordable places I've ever lived. I moved here from Miami as part of a long-term plan since Miami is slowly sinking under the ocean. Lexington has become my home where I garden for a living and help others garden too.

explicit_text: I grew up in Miami, Florida, specifically in a neighborhood called Opa-locka. Miami was my childhood home, but I chose to leave because of environmental concerns like the city sinking under the ocean. Miami is also a major hub for the Jamaican diaspora, which connects to my family's migration story.

explicit_text: Opa-locka is where I grew up, and it's a fascinating place because it was a planned city themed on the Arabian Nights stories, also known as One Thousand and One Nights. The city has lots of Arabian-style buildings, domes, and minarets, and I lived on Arabia Avenue. It was developed in the 1920s by Glenn Curtiss and architects from New York City to attract northerners to Florida.

It was a unique cultural influence on my childhood environment.

explicit_text: Glenn Curtiss was a famous aviator in the 1920s who played a key role in developing Opa-locka, Florida, as a planned city themed on One Thousand and One Nights. His vision shaped the place where I spent my childhood.
